title: Putranjiva zeylanica
taxonomy: Putranjiva zeylanica is a species of flowering plant in the family Putranjivaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and parts of India. The plant is a member of the order Malpighiales, which includes various families of flowering plants. Putranjiva zeylanica has been classified as a species in the genus Putranjiva, which is further classified in the family Putranjivaceae. The classification of Putranjiva zeylanica reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Putranjiva zeylanica is known for its medicinal properties, which have been utilized in traditional medicine for various purposes. The plant's extracts have been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and dermatitis. Additionally, Putranjiva zeylanica has been used to treat fever and inflammation. Its leaves are also used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat digestive issues. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal uses and potential benefits.

parts: Putranjiva zeylanica, the medicinal parts of this plant include its leaves, bark, and roots. side_effects: Putranjiva zeylanica, also known as the 'paper tree' or 'dreaded tree', is reported to have various side effects due to its toxic properties. The seeds, bark, and roots of the plant contain toxic compounds that can cause skin irritation, allergic reactions, and other health issues. Ingestion of the plant's parts can lead to nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea in some individuals. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can cause skin lesions and irritation.
